founder_p1: Samuel is a business educator, coach, and entrepreneur with over a decade of experience helping founders across three continents turn ideas into operating businesses.
founder_p2: He holds an MBA and an MSc in Economics — bringing academic rigour to a field often dominated by anecdote and hype. Before founding Mind and Method Academy, Samuel built and scaled a property company managing social housing rentals to over £15,000 in monthly revenue.
founder_p3: Samuel has coached hundreds of aspiring founders, delivered workshops in universities, community centres, and prisons, and built a reputation for combining practical frameworks with genuine accountability. He has worked with Samsung on enterprise development initiatives and continues to partner with organisations committed to widening access to entrepreneurship.
story_p1: The business education market is broken in two directions. At one end, there are expensive MBA programmes and accelerators designed for people who already have access, capital, and networks. At the other, there is an ocean of free content — YouTube videos, blog posts, social media advice — that creates the illusion of progress without producing results.
story_p2: In the middle are millions of capable people: corporate professionals considering a career change, young people with ideas but no structure, individuals from communities where entrepreneurship feels out of reach. They have the talent. They have the work ethic. What they lack is a clear, affordable, and accountable path from where they are to where they want to be.
story_p3: Mind and Method Academy was built to be that path. World-class business education, without the £30,000 MBA. Structured programmes that produce founders, not just students.
